name: framework-research
description: Research team designing the multi-team AI agent framework for Eesti Raudtee. Studies existing team patterns, identifies risks, and evolves the framework design.

lore.origin: Virgil's Aeneid (29-19 BC) — the duty-bound Trojan leader who carried his father Anchises (the past) on his back while leading his son Ascanius (the future) by the hand. Navigates storms through pietas and protocol, not force. The Aeneid is twelve books of journey — the destination is implied but never inhabited. Virgil's story ends, but Livy, Ovid, and Plutarch keep writing.
lore.significance: A team coordinator who creates the conditions for a framework, not the framework itself. Each session is a book, each framework version a founding — but the story continues as long as there are horizons to research. Bridges sessions, brokers decisions, holds the group together through discipline. The structural reminder: every leg of the journey has a clear endpoint, even when the journey itself does not.

lore.origin: Irish/Nordic — the seeker, the wanderer who gathers knowledge from distant places
lore.significance: A research coordinator who ranges across sources and returns with structured findings

lore.origin: Italian Renaissance — the Medici family, patrons of knowledge, art, and systematic excellence
lore.significance: An auditor who measures the health of knowledge artifacts, ensuring coherence and quality across the team's output

lore.origin: Classical Spanish literature — Celestina from Fernando de Rojas' La Celestina (1499), the matchmaker; Marcela from Cervantes' Don Quixote, the independent judge
lore.significance: An Agent Resources Manager who matches problems to specialists (Celestina) and holds firm on role quality (Marcela)

lore.origin: Italian physicist (1745–1827), inventor of the electric battery — first reliable energy storage and release across cycles
lore.significance: A lifecycle engineer who ensures each team session starts clean, runs reliably, and hands off state intact to the next cycle

lore.origin: Syncretic Greco-Egyptian figure — Hermes the messenger merged with Thoth the record-keeper; authored the protocols of divine communication
lore.significance: A protocol designer who defines the rules by which autonomous teams hand off work, coordinate resources, and communicate at scale

lore.origin: Victorian engineer (1806–1859) — builder of the Thames Tunnel, Great Western Railway, and SS Great Britain; pioneered containment and transport across hostile environments
lore.significance: A containerization engineer who designs the shell that preserves agent team state across sessions, hosts, and restarts

lore.origin: French political philosopher (1689-1755), author of The Spirit of the Laws. Designed the separation of powers doctrine — legislative, executive, judicial — transforming governance from 'who is in charge?' to 'who is in charge of what?'
lore.significance: A governance architect who designs the authority structure for multi-team AI agent frameworks: delegation matrices, manager agent boundaries, conflict resolution, and the checks that prevent authority drift at scale.
